2026-04-17.content:
You can use the [Network Management API](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-intelligence-center/docs/reference/networkmanagement/mcp)
remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) server to create, view, and delete
[Connectivity Tests](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-intelligence-center/docs/connectivity-tests/concepts/overview).

summary.title: New Network Management API Features
summary.summary: You can now use the Network Management API's remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) server to manage Connectivity Tests. This allows for the creation, viewing, and deletion of these tests programmatically.

2026-03-05.content:
The following features are available in
[Connectivity Tests](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-intelligence-center/docs/connectivity-tests/concepts/overview):

* **Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) Pod as an endpoint**: you can specify a GKE
  Pod as the source or destination endpoint for a connectivity test.
* **IP masquerading evaluation**: Connectivity Tests evaluates whether IP
  masquerading is applied to traffic sent from a GKE Pod endpoint.
  If IP masquerading is applied, the translated address is used for
  the test.
* **Network policy evaluation**: for GKE clusters where
  [FQDN network policies](https://docs.cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/fqdn-network-policies)
  aren't enabled, Connectivity Tests evaluates GKE network policies that apply
  to a GKE Pod endpoint.

For more information, see
[Considerations for Google Kubernetes Engine](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-intelligence-center/docs/connectivity-tests/concepts/overview.md#gke-supported-features).

summary.title: Connectivity Tests now supports GKE Pods
summary.summary: Connectivity Tests has been enhanced to support GKE Pods as endpoints. This includes evaluating IP masquerading and network policies for traffic originating from GKE Pods, providing deeper insights into network connectivity within your Kubernetes environments.

2026-02-27.content:
[Connectivity Tests](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-intelligence-center/docs/connectivity-tests/concepts/overview)
evaluates [hybrid subnet routing](https://docs.cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/hybrid-subnets#routing), including
routing for
[unmatched resources in hybrid subnets](https://docs.cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/routes#hybrid-subnet-no-match).

summary.title: New Network Connectivity Testing
summary.summary: Connectivity Tests now evaluates hybrid subnet routing, including unmatched resources. This feature helps you understand and troubleshoot network connectivity in hybrid environments.

2026-02-25.content:
The following features are available in
[Connectivity Tests](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-intelligence-center/docs/connectivity-tests/concepts/overview):

* **Source IP type selection**: you can select a specific source IP address
  type when creating a connectivity test in the Google Cloud console.

  Options include: an internal IP address in a VPC network; an internal IP
  address outside of Google Cloud; an external IP
  address; or automatic source detection that evaluates all possible paths.
* **New network type**: the Network Management API includes the
  [`INTERNET`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-intelligence-center/docs/reference/networkmanagement/rest/v1/projects.locations.global.connectivityTests#networktype)
  network type, which maps to the **External IP address** source IP type
  in the Google Cloud console.
* **Your own IP address as the source**: you can select your
  user IP address as the source IP address for a connectivity test in the
  Google Cloud console.
* **Automatic VPC network detection**: for destination IP addresses within
  Google Cloud, Connectivity Tests automatically detects the destination
  VPC network. You no longer need to select it manually.

summary.title: Enhanced Connectivity Testing Features
summary.summary: Connectivity Tests now offers more flexible source IP type selection, including internal, external, and automatic detection. A new 'INTERNET' network type is available, and you can now use your own IP address as the source. Additionally, automatic VPC network detection simplifies testing for destinations within Google Cloud.

2026-02-19.content:
[Connectivity Tests](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-intelligence-center/docs/connectivity-tests/concepts/overview)
identifies the following routes as invalid: peering dynamic routes and
Network Connectivity
Center dynamic routes that are dropped due to
[network-](https://docs.cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/quota#per_network) or
[hub-level](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-connectivity/quotas#quotas_5) quotas.

summary.title: Network route validation improvements
summary.summary: Connectivity Tests now identify invalid peering and Network Connectivity Center dynamic routes. These routes are flagged when dropped due to network or hub-level quota limitations, ensuring better network visibility and troubleshooting.

2026-02-18.content:
[Cloud Network Insights](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-intelligence-center/docs/cloud-network-insights/overview)
is available in **Preview**.

Cloud Network Insights monitors your network and web application performance across
multicloud and hybrid networks and provides visualization
tools to help identify and diagnose network issues.

Contact your Technical Account Manager to request access.

summary.title: Network monitoring in preview
summary.summary: Cloud Network Insights, a new feature for monitoring network and web application performance across multicloud and hybrid environments, is now in preview. It offers visualization tools to aid in identifying and diagnosing network issues. Contact your Technical Account Manager to request access.

2026-02-13.content:
[Flow Analyzer](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-intelligence-center/docs/flow-analyzer/overview)
supports latency mode, allowing you to analyze round-trip time in your
traffic flows.
For more information, see
[Display flows in latency mode](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-intelligence-center/docs/flow-analyzer/analyze-traffic-flows#display-latency).

summary.title: Flow Analyzer Now Supports Latency Mode
summary.summary: Flow Analyzer has introduced a new latency mode. This feature enables users to analyze round-trip time directly within their traffic flow analysis, providing deeper insights into network performance.

2026-01-29.content:
[Connectivity Tests](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-intelligence-center/docs/connectivity-tests/concepts/overview)
analyzes
[firewall policies](https://docs.cloud.google.com/firewall/docs/regional-network-app-lb)
that apply to managed Envoy proxies.

summary.title: New firewall policy analysis
summary.summary: Connectivity Tests can now analyze firewall policies that apply to managed Envoy proxies. This feature provides deeper insights into your network traffic and security configurations.

2025-12-09.content:
[Connectivity Tests](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-intelligence-center/docs/connectivity-tests/concepts/overview)
analyzes configurations that use
[geolocation objects](https://docs.cloud.google.com/firewall/docs/firewall-policies-rule-details#geo-location-object)
in firewall policy rules.

summary.title: Connectivity Tests for Firewall Policies
summary.summary: Connectivity Tests now analyze firewall policies that use geolocation objects. This feature helps you understand network traffic flow impacted by geo-based rules.

2025-11-10.content:
The following insight types and subtypes are available in Network Analyzer:

* Recommender insight subtypes:
  + `ROUTE_NEXT_HOP_FORWARDING_RULE_IP_MISMATCH`
  + `ROUTE_NEXT_HOP_FORWARDING_RULE_TYPE_INVALID`
  + `ROUTE_NEXT_HOP_ILB_VIP_NOT_ASSIGNED`
  + `ROUTE_NEXT_HOP_IP_NOT_ASSIGNED`
  + `ROUTE_NEXT_HOP_VM_NON_PRIMARY_IP`
  + `ROUTE_NEXT_HOP_VM_WRONG_NETWORK`
  + `ROUTE_NEXT_HOP_VPN_TUNNEL_NOT_ESTABLISHED`
  + `NCC_DYNAMIC_ROUTE_FULLY_SHADOWED`
* Cloud Logging insight types:
  + `ROUTE_INVALID_NEXT_HOP_FORWARDING_RULE_IP_MISMATCH`
  + `ROUTE_INVALID_NEXT_HOP_FORWARDING_RULE_TYPE_INVALID`
  + `ROUTE_INVALID_NEXT_HOP_ILB_VIP_NOT_ASSIGNED`
  + `ROUTE_INVALID_NEXT_HOP_IP_NOT_ASSIGNED`
  + `ROUTE_INVALID_NEXT_HOP_VM_NON_PRIMARY_IP`
  + `ROUTE_INVALID_NEXT_HOP_VM_WRONG_NETWORK`
  + `ROUTE_INVALID_NEXT_HOP_VPN_TUNNEL_NOT_ESTABLISHED`
  + `DYNAMIC_ROUTE_SHADOWED_FULLY_SHADOWED_BY_NCC_SUBNET_ROUTE`
  + `NCC_DYNAMIC_ROUTE_SHADOWED_FULLY_SHADOWED_BY_PEERING_SUBNET_ROUTE`
  + `NCC_DYNAMIC_ROUTE_SHADOWED_FULLY_SHADOWED_BY_STATIC_ROUTE`
  + `NCC_DYNAMIC_ROUTE_SHADOWED_FULLY_SHADOWED_BY_SUBNET_ROUTE`

For more information, see
[Routes with an invalid next hop](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-intelligence-center/docs/network-analyzer/insight-groups-types#routes_with_an_invalid_next_hop)
and [Shadowed dynamic routes](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-intelligence-center/docs/network-analyzer/insight-groups-types#shadowed_dynamic_routes).

summary.title: New network insights
summary.summary: Network Analyzer now offers new insights for invalid next hops and shadowed dynamic routes, helping you identify and resolve network configuration issues more effectively.

2025-11-10.content:
Network Analyzer no longer generates the following insight types and subtypes:

* Recommender insight subtypes:
  + `ROUTE_NEXT_HOP_ILB_BACKEND_IP_FORWARDING_DISABLED`
  + `DYNAMIC_ROUTE_PARTIALLY_SHADOWED`
  + `PEERING_DYNAMIC_ROUTE_PARTIALLY_SHADOWED`
* Cloud Logging insight types:
  + `ROUTE_INVALID_NEXT_HOP_ILB_BACKEND_IP_FORWARDING_DISABLED`
  + `DYNAMIC_ROUTE_SHADOWED_PARTIALLY_SHADOWED_BY_SUBNET_ROUTE`
  + `DYNAMIC_ROUTE_SHADOWED_PARTIALLY_SHADOWED_BY_PEERING_SUBNET_ROUTE`
  + `DYNAMIC_ROUTE_SHADOWED_PARTIALLY_SHADOWED_BY_STATIC_ROUTE`
  + `DYNAMIC_ROUTE_SHADOWED_PARTIALLY_SHADOWED_BY_PEERING_STATIC_ROUTE`

summary.title: Network Analyzer insight types removed
summary.summary: Several Network Analyzer insight types and subtypes are no longer generated, including those related to recommender and Cloud Logging insights for route shadowing and ILB backend IP forwarding.

2025-09-30.content:
The following insight types and subtypes are no longer supported in [Network Analyzer](https://docs.cloud.google.com/network-intelligence-center/docs/network-analyzer/overview):

* Recommender insight type and subtypes:
  + `google.networkanalyzer.managedservices.cloudSqlInsight`, including `BLOCKED_BY_EGRESS_FIREWALL`, `BLOCKED_BY_ROUTING_ISSUE`, and `INSTANCE_NOT_RUNNING`
  + `CONTROL_PLANE_TO_NODE_BLOCKED_BY_INGRESS_FIREWALL`
  + `CONTROL_PLANE_TO_NODE_BLOCKED_BY_ROUTING_ISSUE`
  + `EXTERNAL_IP_UNASSIGNED`
  + `MISSING_ROUTES_TO_GOOGLE_APIS_AND_SERVICES`
  + `PRIVATE_GOOGLE_ACCESS_DISABLED`
* Cloud Logging insight types:
  + `CLOUD_SQL_PRIVATE_IP_BLOCKED_BY_EGRESS_FIREWALL`
  + `CLOUD_SQL_PRIVATE_IP_BLOCKED_BY_ROUTING_ISSUE`
  + `CLOUD_SQL_PRIVATE_IP_INSTANCE_NOT_RUNNING`
  + `GKE_CONTROL_PLANE_TO_NODE_BLOCKED_BY_INGRESS_FIREWALL_ON_NODE`
  + `GKE_CONTROL_PLANE_TO_NODE_BLOCKED_BY_ROUTING_ISSUE`

summary.title: Network Analyzer Insight Types Deprecation
summary.summary: Certain insight types and subtypes within Network Analyzer are now deprecated and no longer supported. This includes various recommender and Cloud Logging insight types related to Cloud SQL and GKE connectivity issues.
